content: We can reframe curiosity-driven autonomous agents to use tool-based memory operations as their core intrinsic reward signal: curious agents earn intrinsic reward not just for encountering novel observation patterns, but for successfully executing structured tool-based edits to their own long-term memory that improve future retrieval and active learning performance. This creates a self-reinforcing loop where an agent’s exploration is directly targeted at growing the functional utility of its own memory, rather than just seeking out unfamiliar sensory input.
